invited inside the home of a Shia-Kurd family. The head of household informs me that Baathists are still killing people, and Maliki is good.
Indirect fire at 200 meters; black smoke rises behind a building. in front of the building, traffic flows as though not much had happened.
returned after some rest outside. after a month here, the mind turns to cheese. our soldiers put up with far worse. they are everday heroes
helicopter roars overhead, close enough to set off car alarms on the ground. Tomorrow depart on my R&R. One more cup of coffee, then I'll go
